item.text: I found two clear local corrections in the residual-risk audit: unreadable verifier-report paths currently fall through as generic command errors, and the closeout teaching/prompt language can be more explicit about “no skip path for synthesis” and cross-checking synthesis against the codebase. I’m tightening both now and adding tests so those boundary conditions stay locked.

The residual-risk fixes are in. I’m regenerating the embedded prompt assets and then I’ll run the focused Story 8 tests that touch the synthesizer boundary, prompt contract, and closeout teaching before the full gate.

item.text:
**findings fixed**
- `P3-01`: `epic-synthesize` now treats unreadable verifier-report inputs as a structured blocked spec-pack error instead of falling through to a generic command failure. Files changed: [processes/impl-cli/core/epic-synthesizer.ts](/Users/leemoore/code/liminal-spec/processes/impl-cli/core/epic-synthesizer.ts), [processes/impl-cli/tests/epic-synthesize-command.test.ts](/Users/leemoore/code/liminal-spec/processes/impl-cli/tests/epic-synthesize-command.test.ts)
- `P3-02`: strengthened AC-8.3/closeout teaching so synthesis explicitly cross-checks against the codebase and the playbook explicitly forbids a skip path around synthesis. Files changed: [processes/impl-cli/prompts/base/epic-synthesizer.md](/Users/leemoore/code/liminal-spec/processes/impl-cli/prompts/base/epic-synthesizer.md), [src/references/claude-impl-process-playbook.md](/Users/leemoore/code/liminal-spec/src/references/claude-impl-process-playbook.md), [processes/impl-cli/tests/prompt-asset-contract.test.ts](/Users/leemoore/code/liminal-spec/processes/impl-cli/tests/prompt-asset-contract.test.ts), [scripts/__tests__/claude-impl-skill.test.ts](/Users/leemoore/code/liminal-spec/scripts/__tests__/claude-impl-skill.test.ts), [processes/impl-cli/core/embedded-assets.generated.ts](/Users/leemoore/code/liminal-spec/processes/impl-cli/core/embedded-assets.generated.ts)

**findings surfaced to orchestrator**
- `P3-S1`: Cleanup batch artifact validation is still intentionally loose. Summary: the CLI checks existence/readability and uses an `APPROVED` marker heuristic, so malformed batch markdown can still degrade into a no-op or provider-fed cleanup prompt. Why uncertain: the story says “orchestrator-curated markdown file listing items” but does not define a stricter schema or rejection contract.
- `P3-S2`: Verifier-report inputs still have no explicit size cap. Summary: non-existent and unreadable paths are now handled, but very large report files are not bounded. Why uncertain: the spec asks for a boundary audit but does not define a max size or truncation strategy for synthesis inputs.
